title: Doctrine enrichment envelope
purpose: Define the reader-enrichment envelope that turns a doctrine object (axiom, principle, anti-principle) from a one-line rule row into a drill-down doctrine cell: a deep reading, a formal statement with a symbol table, the problem it governs, what it requires and refuses, a worked example wired to a real component, the tempting mistake, the enforcement routes, and the bound on what it proves.
authority_boundary: Enrichment is reader content, not law and not support evidence. The formal_clause source of record stays in core/axiom_organ_routing.json (axioms), PRINCIPLES.md (principles), and ANTI_PRINCIPLES.md (anti-principles); the latex here is a reviewed reader rendering of that clause. The axiom support-cover validator does not read this file, so an example or enforcement route named here never computes support, never raises a claim ceiling, and never becomes anti-axiom rejection. A generated page may expose this content but cannot raise the authority of the source it explains (P-15).

reader_fields.one_line: Compact reading for the card summary; the routing band.
reader_fields.deep: Two to four sentences of load-bearing reading: the substance, not drama. Richer per line than a restated title.

formal.latex: Source LaTeX for the formal statement, within the bounded build-time grammar (no environments, no \not, no underscores inside \mathrm). Renders to offline HTML; out-of-grammar input degrades to code with a recorded render gap.
formal.reads: Plain-language reading of the formula. It must read THIS formula faithfully and must not claim more than the source clause (necessity stated as implication, a definitional criterion as a biconditional).
formal.symbols: List of {sym, meaning} entries. The table must be SOUND with respect to the formula: every free variable and every named operator (\mathrm{...}, \mathcal{...}) used in the latex has an entry, and every declared entry appears in the latex (no dangling symbol). Standard logical / relational / lattice connectives, grouping delimiters, and self-describing \mathsf{...} verdict constants are common vocabulary and need no entry.

reader_fields.governs: The problem or boundary the object governs.
reader_fields.refuses: The shortcut it refuses (the anti-axiom for axioms; the failure shape for anti-principles).

example.text: A positive, source-routed case where the object is enforced or witnessed.
example.refs: Public component ids the example is grounded in.

counterexample.text: The tempting failure the object exists to refuse.

reader_fields.enforced_in: Public component ids where the object is witnessed or governs, rendered as links.
reader_fields.does_not_prove: The explicit bound: what the object and its example do NOT establish.

reader_ladder.plain: One short, literal lay sentence (no jargon, no symbols). A non-expert grasps the point from this alone, before any disclosure opens.

analogy.text: One concrete, familiar everyday analogy that preserves the object's exact core invariant.
analogy.maps: List of {doctrine, analogy} correspondences making the analogy's fidelity inspectable (the anti-laundering map).
analogy.boundary: Where the analogy STOPS: what it does not prove or imply. Mandatory; an analogy without a boundary is a laundering vector.

reader_ladder.why_it_matters: Why a reader should care, in practical terms.

reader_ladder_invariant.statement: The reader ladder is a governed reader primitive, not decoration. Every object carries a plain reading and a bounded analogy. The analogy must preserve the object's core invariant (the maps make that inspectable) and must name its limit (the boundary). An analogy without a boundary is the same laundering failure the doctrine refuses: it lets a vivid metaphor stand in for doctrine the source never carried.
reader_ladder_invariant.accessibility_rules: Lay fields use literal language first, short sentences, no jargon, no symbols, no public-vocab-banned visible terms, and make no proof/guarantee claim in the affirmative fields (plain, analogy.text, why_it_matters); a 'does not prove' disclaimer belongs in the boundary or potential_misread. The analogy is optional support for understanding, never the only explanation and never proof.
reader_ladder_invariant.gate: scripts/check_doctrine_reader_ladder.py (--check exits non-zero on any defect; consumed by build_doctrine_enrichment_health.py and tests/test_doctrine_reader_ladder.py)

formal_soundness_invariant.statement: A formal block is sound when the symbol table and the formula agree (no undefined symbol, no dangling symbol) and the formula does not assert more than the source clause. This is a reader-soundness property of symbol/formula agreement, NOT a proof of mathematical correctness or of axiom support; correctness is reviewed, soundness is gated.
formal_soundness_invariant.gate: scripts/check_doctrine_formal_soundness.py (--check exits non-zero on any defect; consumed by build_doctrine_enrichment_health.py and tests/test_doctrine_formal_soundness.py)
formal_soundness_invariant.source_of_record_ceiling: The latex may not exceed the formal_clause it renders (axiom_organ_routing.json for axioms, PRINCIPLES.md / ANTI_PRINCIPLES.md for principles / anti-principles). Necessity is rendered as implication; only a definitional criterion is rendered as a biconditional.

non_laundering_rules: An example is reader illustration, never a support case; it does not pass the support_case_shape and does not raise support.
An enforced_in route is a navigation aid, not proof that the object is obligation-supported or complete.
The latex rendering is a projection of the source clause and cannot become a competing source of record.
An analogy is reader illustration, never proof; it explains the object and is bounded by an explicit limit, and it never establishes the object or computes support.
Coverage of an enrichment field is presence, not correctness; correctness (formal soundness, analogy fidelity, boundary honesty) is reviewed and gated, not counted.
anti_claims: This standard does not flip source authority for any axiom, principle, or anti-principle.
A fully enriched doctrine object is not therefore a strong or witnessed object; admission, support, and enrichment are independent axes.
Adding enrichment fields is reader grammar, not release or proof authority.
